title: shanku.net resume data
description: Source of truth for /resume/. build.py renders index.html + the ATS PDF from this file. additionalProperties is false everywhere so a typo fails loud instead of rendering wrong. v2 (dynamic-resume-v2): grown to full content depth - 6-role career ledger with bullets, education, certifications, storytelling, signature strengths, real venture bodies, enabled ship log.

summary.description: Professional summary paragraphs, verbatim from the source resume (resume.shanku.net). Rendered as the PDF's Summary section. The site rail uses identity.oneliner (the punchy tagline) instead.

last_updated.description: Set by whoever edits this file. NEVER datetime.now() in build.py - determinism is load-bearing for --check.

motto.description: Rail ceiling: build.py enforces <=40 chars. A two-line motto changes the rail math the design was verified against.

avatar.description: Root-relative. The page lives at /resume/, so relative image paths 404.

mbar_cred.description: Mobile sticky-bar credential. Short by design: iLink · ex-Microsoft.

contact.description: Rendered as a dedicated block under the Download button (rail + mobile identity), distinct from the credentials ledger, AND into the PDF contact line. decision-v2: contact = how to reach me; credentials = pedigree.

phone.description: Human-formatted, e.g. (912) 484-3825. The tel: href is derived from the digits in build/template. Empty string omits it from the PDF.

linkedin.description: Full https URL used for the link href. Empty string omits it from the PDF.

linkedin_display.description: Short display label for the linkedin link, e.g. linkedin.shanku.net.

credentials.description: Rail ceiling: build.py enforces <=3 rows. A fourth row changes the rail math the design was verified against.

body.description: Real paragraph, not a one-liner. build.py content gate enforces >= 200 chars (EC-4): the v1 failure was one-line placeholder bodies ported as the contract.

field.description: The expanded-body field row. WayFact's missing URL is a designed value, not a gap: kind=status renders [status: private beta] in the same field grammar as every other entry's URL. Never fake a link.

signal.description: The collapsed-row meta slot. Per-venture-type by design: date for shipping ventures, count for iLink Tools, status chip for WayFact.

display.description: ledger = the stacked two-line total (unique to iLink Tools by design); inline = single-line 'N caption'.

embed.description: Video id source. The facade renders a thumbnail link-out to the watch URL; never an iframe in the DOM.

duration.description: Badge text like 1:56. Empty string omits the badge.

thesis.description: Optional closing ruled block at the end of a venture card. Currently unused.

ai_disclosure.description: Optional lead-in line rendered above the exemplars block. For iLink Tools: discloses the demos are AI-produced (digital twin, cloned voice, agent screen capture, programmatic edit).

storytelling.description: Differentiator section placed before Career (decision-v2). The Moth wins + Speaking statline + mantra.

youtube.description: Full watch URL. Muted grey glyph -> #FF0000 on hover; SVG aria-hidden, link carries accessible label.

stats.description: Direction-B statline. '595+ Reached' is a documented FLOOR (summed only across the 9 engagements that reported a headcount) - the trailing + is the floor marker. Never present as exact (decision-v2 Round 2 #4).

mantra.description: Reuses .thesis/.mantra grammar (gold left-border + rgba wash). Not duplicated elsewhere.

rows.description: Reverse-chronological. Disclosure ledger: each row is a <details>. Recency-weighted bullets - build.py content gate enforces the 3 most-recent roles carry >=3 bullets, condensed roles >=2, every bullet >=60 chars (EC-2).

company_note.description: Optional inline note, e.g. (now Valorem Reply).

tag.description: Optional metadata pill rendered at the end of the bullet (e.g. 'AI for Ops'). Muted chip on the page, '(tag)' parenthetical in the PDF.

signature_strengths.description: Curated Top-5 rated strip above the skill chips (decision-v2 Round 2 #5). Ratifiable set, not locked. Rendered as 5-segment meters from the score.

score.description: 0.5 steps. Meter renders floor(score) full segments + one half segment when the .5 remainder is present.

skills.description: The 6 source categories: Cloud Platforms, AI/ML, Data & Analytics, Development, Automation, Pre-Sales.

education.description: Rendered inline (.edu-row) in the Credentials section.

certifications.description: Grouped by issuer behind a <details> disclosure in Credentials. Total + issuer count are derived (sum of items, len of groups).

principles.description: Behind a second Credentials disclosure sibling to Certifications. Core values, influential books, kept quotes.

enabled.description: v2: true. The sweep+publisher populate entries from real repo evidence; each entry carries a source_commits provenance trail.

entries.description: Reverse-chronological. entries[0] is most recent - the publisher inserts new entries at index 0.

source_commits.description: Provenance trail written by the sweep/publisher: which repo commits produced this entry. EC-6 requires >=1 published entry to carry a non-empty trail.

automated.description: Hard-pinned false. The 16 is a hand-curated editorial roster that is NOT derivable from tools.json. build.py's ledger gate asserts this. The sweep must NEVER touch it. See TheGrove wiki/decisions/ad192.md before touching.
